intro.uz: Repetition — mahoratga eltadigan yagona yoʻl: bir ishni kuch va eʼtibor bilan qayta-qayta takrorlash (repetition with intensity), har safar kichik yangi farq (distinction) qoʻshib. Bu haftaning ikki grammatik mavzusi shu gʻoyaga bogʻlangan: oʻtmish odatlari — used to / would (eski tiqishtirib yodlashni yangi kunlik mashq bilan solishtiramiz) va payt predloglari — for / since / during / ago (takror doim vaqt bilan oʻlchanadi).
intro.en: Repetition is the only road to mastery: doing something again and again with power and focus (repetition with intensity), adding a small new distinction each time. This week's two grammar topics build on that idea: past habits — used to / would (contrasting old cramming with new daily practice) and time prepositions — for / since / during / ago (repetition is always measured in time).

canDo.uz: Dars oxirida oʻtmishda nima qilgan yoki qanday boʻlgan boʻlishingizni (used to / would) toʻxtamasdan ayta olasiz va biror ishni qancha vaqt, qachondan beri yoki qachon qilganingizni (for / since / during / ago) toʻgʻri ayta olasiz.
canDo.en: By the end you can say what you used to do or how you used to be (used to / would) without pausing, and say how long, since when, or when you did something (for / since / during / ago) correctly.

grammar.titleEn: Past habits & states — used to / would
grammar.bandLifter: IELTS Part 2 — talk about how your life or habits used to be different
grammar.cefrCanDo: B1: I can describe past habits and states that are no longer true (used to / would)
grammar.contrastUz: Oʻzbek tilida buni “-ar edi / -(a)digan edi” bilan aytamiz: “chalar edi”, “uyatchan edi”. Bu qolip oʻzbekchada harakatni ham, holatni ham koʻtaraveradi. Ingliz tilida esa chiziq aniq: takrorlangan HARAKAT uchun “used to” yoki “would”; HOLAT (be, have, like, know) uchun esa faqat “used to” — “would” emas.
grammar.errorFixUz: ❌ I use to study all night. He would be lazy. ✅ I used to study all night. He used to be lazy. Savol/inkorda “d” tushadi: Did you use to…? / I didn’t use to… . Holat uchun hech qachon “would” emas — faqat “used to”.

examples.en: I used to cram before exams, but now I practise every day.
examples.uz: Ilgari imtihon oldidan tiqishtirib yodlardim, endi esa har kuni mashq qilaman.

examples.en: When he was young, he would practise the guitar for hours.
She used to be very shy.
examples.uz: U ilgari juda uyatchan edi (hozir emas).

exercises.prompt: I ___ study by cramming, but now I practise every day.
exercises.hintUz: oʻtmishdagi, endi yoʻq odat → used to (tasdiqda “d” bor)

exercises.prompt: Every day AJ ___ repeat the same singing scales.
Asher ___ be terrible at the guitar.
exercises.hintUz: HOLAT (be) → faqat used to; “would” holat uchun ishlamaydi

exercises.promptUz: Oʻzingiz haqingizda rost gap ayting (ovoz chiqarib): “I used to … , but now I …” va “When I was a child, I would … .”
exercises.hintUz: used to = odat yoki holat; would = faqat takrorlangan harakat

grammar.titleUz: Payt predloglari: for / since / during / ago
grammar.titleEn: Time & duration prepositions — for / since / during / ago
grammar.bandLifter: IELTS Part 1 — say how long and when accurately
grammar.cefrCanDo: B1: I can use for / since / during / ago correctly with the right tense
grammar.contrastUz: Oʻzbekchada bitta soʻz bir nechta maʼnoni koʻtaradi: “beri” = since, “davomida / mobaynida” = for yoki during, “oldin” = ago. Ingliz tilida har biri qatʼiy: for = QANCHA vaqt (davomiylik), since = QACHONDAN beri (boshlanish nuqtasi), during = biror davr ICHIDA (+ ot), ago = hozirdan ORQAGA sanab (+ oddiy oʻtgan zamon).
grammar.errorFixUz: ❌ for 2020 / since three years / before two years / during two hours. ✅ since 2020 / for three years / two years ago / for two hours. Raqamli davomiylik → for; sana/nuqta → since; “… oldin” → before emas, ago.

examples.en: Michael Jordan practised the same skills for twenty years.
examples.uz: Michael Jordan oʻsha mahoratlarni yigirma yil mashq qildi.

examples.en: He has played the guitar since 2019.
Asher became a great guitarist three years ago.
examples.uz: Asher uch yil oldin zoʻr gitarachi boʻldi.

exercises.prompt: I have studied English ___ three years.

exercises.hintUz: hozirdan orqaga sanoq → time + ago (oddiy oʻtgan zamon bilan)

exercises.promptUz: Oʻzingiz haqingizda rost gap ayting: “I have studied English for … , since … . I started … ago.”
exercises.hintUz: for = davomiylik, since = nuqta, ago = orqaga sanoq

vocab.uz: kuch va eʼtibor bilan qayta-qayta takrorlash
vocab.defEn: doing something again and again with concentrated energy and focus
vocab.example: Mastery comes from repetition with intensity, not just listening once.
Each time you repeat, focus on a new distinction.
Practise with intensity, not with laziness.

vocab.defEn: to copy someone very closely and exactly
vocab.example: Don't just repeat — imitate my intonation like an actor.

vocab.defEn: the act of copying someone exactly
vocab.example: Imitation is the deepest level of repetition.

vocab.uz: chuqur oʻzlashtirish (bir marta olib, hech qachon yoʻqotmaslik)
vocab.defEn: learning something so deeply that you keep it forever
vocab.example: Real knowing means deep acquisition, not memorising for a test.

vocab.defEn: the core, most frequent, basic parts of the language
vocab.example: Always keep practising the fundamentals — the high-frequency words.

vocab.defEn: the music of a language — when the voice rises and falls
vocab.example: Copy my intonation, not just the words.

vocab.defEn: to look for and find someone or something
vocab.example: It's vital to seek out a great teacher to model.

warmup.uz: Suhbatda mijoz telefon orqali pitsa buyurtma qiladi. Sotuvchi “ikkinchi pitsa ham birinchisiga oʻxshash boʻlsinmi?” deb soʻraydi — mijoz esa xuddi shu buyurtmani takrorlaydi, lekin kichik farq (boshqa qoʻshimchalar, thin crust) qoʻshadi. Bu — AJning gʻoyasi: takror, ammo har safar yangi distinction. Sizningcha, buyurtma oʻz vaqtida yetib boradimi?
warmup.en: In this dialogue a customer phones in a pizza order. The seller asks, “same as the first?” — and the customer repeats the order but adds a small difference (new toppings, thin crust). That's AJ's idea: repetition, but with a new distinction each time. Do you think the order will arrive on time?

keyVocab.defEn: an average size, not too big or small
the complete amount you must pay
seems or appears to be okay; I agree
a spicy sausage usually put on pizza
a small, egg-shaped black or green fruit used as food or for making oil
the border or edge of a pizza, or of bread
pay for one and get another one free

quiz.q: According to the BBC Food website, which type of food supports good memory function?
quiz.qUz: BBC Food saytiga koʻra, qaysi turdagi ovqat xotira ishlashini yaxshi qoʻllab-quvvatlaydi?
quiz.explanationUz: Toʻgʻri javob: c) hammasi. Rob b) spinach (ismaloq) dedi, lekin Neil aytdi: aslida uchalasi ham toʻgʻri — tuxum, ismaloq va banan B guruh vitaminlariga boy boʻlib, xotirani qoʻllab-quvvatlaydi.

vocab.defEn: things we remember from the past

vocab.uz: harakat orqali oʻrganish (biror ishni qayta-qayta bajarib)
vocab.defEn: learning by ‘doing’ and practising something over and over again
seeing something in the situation where it usually exists
changing information into a form that can be stored and later recalled
doing something based on feelings rather than facts or proof

vocab.uz: Altsgeymer kasalligi (xotirani susaytiruvchi, yoshga qarab kuchayadigan miya kasalligi)
vocab.defEn: a disease affecting the brain that makes it difficult to remember things and gets worse with age

funEnglish.title: Why repetition is the secret to speaking English fluently

speakingPrompt.en: How did you use to study English, and how has that changed? How long have you been learning now, and what would you do differently a few years ago?
speakingPrompt.uz: Taxminan 60 soniya gapiring. Bu haftaning ikki grammatikasidan foydalaning: oʻtmish odatlari (I used to … ; when I was young I would …) va payt predloglari (for / since / during / … ago).
